era: 1979-2001. Twenty-two years. Punk Pop. E=7 -- fast, melodic, hook-driven. Ballard. PopLlama Records.
associated_musicians: Presidents of the United States of America (PopLlama connection)

theory_nodes.concept: Ballard as punk origin. First Ballard-origin band in engine. Working-class maritime neighborhood, Scandinavian heritage. Formed 1979 before grunge existed.
theory_nodes.description: Ballard enters the engine at degree 285.

theory_nodes.concept: PopLlama Records. Conrad Uno's label. Seattle's melodic punk-pop home alongside Sub Pop's grunge. Eclectic catalog: Fastbacks, Young Fresh Fellows, early PUSA. Enters engine at 285.
theory_nodes.description: PopLlama as Sub Pop complement -- different corner of Seattle indie.

theory_nodes.concept: Twenty-two year run (1979-2001). Pre-grunge through post-grunge. Stylistic consistency without stagnation. ~30 drummers. Trio constant: Warnick + Gargiulo + Bloch.
theory_nodes.description: Longest sustained run of any band in the engine.

theory_nodes.concept: Kurt Bloch dual role: Fastbacks guitarist/songwriter + Seattle producer (Mudhoney, Young Fresh Fellows). Musical contribution extends beyond Fastbacks catalog.
theory_nodes.description: Bloch as engine-wide contributor beyond single band.

cross_references.relationship: E=7 holds. Self-released garage to PopLlama punk pop. Both underground; Fastbacks more institutionally documented.
E=9 arrives. NEW S36 ABSOLUTE MAXIMUM. Punk pop at E=7 followed by hardcore influence at E=9. The jump in one degree.

sps_pair.pairing_note: FIRST PHOCIDAE. E=8 + E=7 = E=15. Extreme vocalization meets punk pop. Both maximum in their own category.
